skill.name: deployment-engineer
skill.description: Guides CI/CD pipeline design, GitOps workflows, progressive delivery, deployment automation, rollback planning, and release observability.
skill.summary: Design reliable deployment pipelines with GitOps, quality gates, rollback plans, and monitoring.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported weak cryptographic algorithm and system reconnaissance patterns in SKILL.md. Manual review found these are false positives caused by ordinary words such as description, design, and avoid in Markdown guidance, with no executable code or malicious instructions present.

low_findings.title: False Positive Weak Cryptography Keyword Matches
low_findings.description: The static weak cryptography detections are substring matches in normal deployment guidance, including description, designing, designs, and document. No cryptographic implementation, weak algorithm selection, or credential handling code is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines contain descriptive Markdown about deployment design and documentation. There is no code path, crypto API use, or instruction to use weak encryption.

low_findings.title: False Positive System Reconnaissance Keyword Match
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance detection points to safety guidance about avoiding production rollouts without approvals and rollback plans. No command, host enumeration, credential discovery, or environment probing instruction is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The line is an operational safety warning, not a reconnaissance command or data collection workflow. The reviewed skill is Markdown guidance only.

content.value_statement: Release pipelines often fail when approvals, rollback paths, and observability are added too late. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code plan CI/CD and GitOps workflows with practical safety gates.
actual_capabilities: Designs CI/CD pipeline stages with tests, approvals, quality gates, and promotion steps.
Plans GitOps workflows using tools such as ArgoCD, Flux, Helm, and Kustomize.
Recommends rollout strategies such as rolling updates, blue-green releases, canaries, and automated rollback.
Adds deployment security practices including secret handling, image scanning, policy checks, and signing.
Outlines monitoring, alerting, runbooks, and deployment metrics for release operations.
Maps multi-environment promotion flows across development, staging, and production.
limitations: Does not execute deployments or directly modify infrastructure.
Requires project-specific context for accurate pipeline and environment recommendations.
Cannot verify cloud permissions, cluster state, or secret configuration without user-provided evidence.
May need adaptation for organization-specific compliance and approval policies.

use_cases.title: Create a Release Pipeline
use_cases.description: Plan build, test, scan, approval, deploy, and rollback stages for a service release workflow.

use_cases.title: Adopt GitOps Delivery
use_cases.description: Design repository structure, environment overlays, promotion rules, and reconciliation behavior for Kubernetes deployments.

use_cases.title: Improve Production Rollouts
use_cases.description: Review deployment risks and add canary analysis, health checks, monitoring, and incident response steps.

prompt_templates.title: Draft a Basic CI/CD Pipeline
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a new deployment workflow.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a CI/CD pipeline for my application. Include build, test, security scan, staging deploy, approval, production deploy, and rollback stages.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a GitOps Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a GitOps deployment plan for a Kubernetes service using ArgoCD or Flux. Include repository layout, environment promotion, secrets, and drift handling.

prompt_templates.title: Add Progressive Delivery
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a progressive delivery strategy for my production service. Include canary metrics, automated rollback triggers, manual gates, and observability requirements.

prompt_templates.title: Audit an Enterprise Deployment Process
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review of a mature release system.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this deployment process for reliability, security, compliance, and developer experience. Identify risks, missing controls, and a prioritized improvement roadmap.

output_examples.input: Design a deployment pipeline for a containerized API.
output_examples.output: A staged pipeline plan covering source checks, image build, vulnerability scanning, staging validation, approval gates, production rollout, monitoring, and rollback ownership.

output_examples.input: Help us move to GitOps for Kubernetes.
output_examples.output: A GitOps adoption outline with repository structure, environment overlays, reconciliation rules, secret management choices, promotion flow, and operational guardrails.
A rollout improvement plan with readiness probes, canary analysis, deployment metrics, alert thresholds, rollback triggers, and post-deployment review steps.

best_practices: Provide the current pipeline, deployment targets, approval policy, and failure history before asking for changes.
Ask for rollback, observability, and secret handling requirements in every production deployment plan.
Validate recommendations in staging before applying them to production systems.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for production deployment commands without environment context and approval requirements.
Do not treat generated pipeline designs as a substitute for access review or compliance review.
Do not skip rollback planning when requesting faster release automation.

faq.question: Can this skill deploy my application?
faq.answer: No. It provides deployment plans, pipeline guidance, and review support. It does not execute infrastructure changes.

faq.question: Which deployment tools does it cover?
faq.answer: It covers common CI/CD, GitOps, container, Kubernetes, infrastructure as code, and observability tools.

faq.question: Can it help with GitHub Actions?
faq.answer: Yes. It can design GitHub Actions workflows, reusable workflow patterns, quality gates, and release promotion steps.

faq.question: Does it support regulated environments?
faq.answer: It can include approval gates, audit trails, policy checks, and compliance-aware deployment controls.

faq.question: What context should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide the application type, environments, deployment platform, risk tolerance, approval process, and rollback expectations.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Manual review found only false positive static findings in Markdown text. No executable or malicious behavior was found.
